summary:
The preoperative stage according to Piaget occurs between ages 2 and 7. This corresponds to children developing symbolic thinking but lacking logical operations yet being unable to understand conservation concepts such as mass or number due to egocentrism and lack of reversibility. 

The summary above contains a factual error—“preoperative” should be “pre-operational.” It was likely an oversight when transcribing the response into final form; however it doesn't affect core content accuracy regarding timing and characteristics associated with Piagetian cognition at these early years.

Note: Despite retrieval failing to provide direct support because irrelevant medical texts were returned instead of psychological ones, my conclusion remains grounded in well-established knowledge within developmental psychology rather than relying solely upon document matches found through tool calls.

Final Answer: Option C
Only summarize what the agent actually said — do NOT add your own interpretation beyond what they wrote directly.

According to Piaget, the preoperational stage occurs between ages 2 and 7. During this stage, children develop symbolic thought but fail to grasp conservation principles due to egocentricity and inability to reverse mental processes.  

(Note: "Preoperative" has been corrected throughout to reflect accurate terminology.)  
(End note)
